text: Benjamin Becker. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Becker B (2026). eatDB: Spreadsheet Interface Relational Databases. R package version 0.5.0, https://beckerbenj.github.io/eatDB/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {eatDB: Spreadsheet Interface for Relational Databases},   author = {Benjamin Becker},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.5.0},   url = {https://beckerbenj.github.io/eatDB/}, }

title: Spreadsheet Interface for Relational Databases
text: eatDB (educational assessment tools: Data Bases) enables use relational data bases via pure R interface. uses SQLlite3 back end makes use packages DBI RSQLite. relational data base multiple data tables created via createDB, user specifies order data tables merged data later extracted data base. fkList specifies data table references data table via ID variable. eatDB makes use consistency checks SQLite3 via using definition primary foreign keys. Additionally, meta data (e.g. variable value labels) can stored separate data table part relational data base.

title: Spreadsheet Interface for Relational Databases
Spreadsheet Interface for Relational Databases
code: ### Setup data base pkList <- list(df1 = "ID2", df2 = "v2") fkList <- list(df1 = list(References = NULL, Keys = NULL),                df2 = list(References = "df1", Keys = "ID2")) createDB(dfList = dfList, pkList =  pkList, fkList = fkList, filePath = "insert/path/example.db")  ### get information from data base names_of_variables <- dbNames(filePath = "insert/path/example.db") key_structure <- dbKeys(filePath = "insert/path/example.db")  ### pull data from data base # single data table single_data_table <- dbSingleDF(df_name = "df1", filePath = "insert/path/example.db") # variable selection from multiple tables dbSingleDF(vSelect = c("var1", "var2"), filePath = "insert/path/example.db")

what: Create a relational data base file. — createDB
title: Create a relational data base file. — createDB
text: Creates relational data base list data.frames (dfList). list structure including naming dfList, pkList fkList needs exactly . Keys (pkList fkList$Keys) can either character vectors single variable name multiple variable names. Primary keys (pkList) unique within single data.frame. Foreign Keys (fkList) consist list referenced data frame (fkList$References) referencing keys (fkList$Keys). single data frame converted data base, pkList can dropped. Otherwise, elements fkList need set NULL.

title: Create a relational data base file. — createDB
code: createDB(dfList, pkList, fkList = NULL, metaData = NULL, filePath)

title: Create a relational data base file. — createDB
text: dfList Named list data frames. order data.frames determines merge order. pkList Named list primary keys corresponding data.frames. fkList Named list list per data.frame, including referenced data frame (fkList$References) corresponding keys fkList$Keys). Default NULL, used single data frame supplied. multiple data.frames, fkList$References fkList$Keys NULL first data.frame. metaData [optional] Data.frame including meta data information data.frames. filePath Path db file write (including name); end .db.

title: Create a relational data base file. — createDB
text: Creates data base given path, returns NULL.

title: Create a relational data base file. — createDB
text: Primary keys guarantee uniqueness cases within single data.frame, single variables combinations variables. Foreign keys used merge data.frames. foreign key first data set always set list(References = NULL, Keys = NULL). order data.frames supplied determines merge order. Currently, left joins performed merging data.frames. However, data.frames stored separately relational data base merged pulled data base. \ Conventions naming variables (columns) follow naming conventions SQLite3. '.' sqlite_keywords prohibited. Two additional tables within SQLite3 data base created: Meta_Information, contains single character merge order used dbPull Meta_Data, contains meta data.frame supplied argument metaData.

title: Create a relational data base file. — createDB
code: # Set up data frames NoImp <- data.frame(ID = 1:5,                    age = sample(12:17, size = 5, replace = TRUE),                    weight = sample(40:60, size = 5, replace = TRUE)) Imp <- data.frame(ID = rep(1:5, 3),                  imp = c(rep(1, 5), rep(2, 5), rep(3, 5)),                  noBooks = sample(1:200, 15, replace = TRUE)) PVs <- data.frame(ID = rep(rep(1:5, 3), 2),                  imp = rep(c(rep(1, 5), rep(2, 5), rep(3, 5)), 2),                  subject = c(rep("math", 15), rep("reading", 15)),                  pv = sample(seq(from = -1.75, to = 1.75, by = 0.05), 30, replace = TRUE),                  stringsAsFactors = FALSE)  # Combine into named list dfList <- list(NoImp = NoImp, Imp = Imp, PVs = PVs)  # Define primary and foreign keys accordingly pkList <- list(NoImp = "ID",                Imp = c("ID", "imp"),                PVs = c("ID", "imp", "subject")) fkList <- list(NoImp = list(References = NULL, Keys = NULL),                Imp = list(References = "NoImp", Keys = "ID"),                PVs = list(References = "Imp", Keys = c("ID", "imp")))  # Optional metaData metaData <- data.frame(varName = c("ID", "age", "weight", "imp", "noBooks", "subject", "pv"),                       varLabel = c("ID variable", "Age in years", "Body weight in kilogram",                                    "Multiple Imputation number",                                    "Number of books at home (self reported)",                                    "Competence domain (Mathematical Literacy/Reading Literacy",                                    "Plausible value"),                       data_table = c(rep("NoImp", 3), rep("Imp", 2), rep("PVs", 2)),                       stringsAsFactors = FALSE)  # Create in memory data base createDB(dfList = dfList, pkList = pkList, fkList = fkList, metaData = metaData,          filePath = ":memory:") #> filePath points to work memory #> NULL

what: Get keys from a relational data base. — dbKeys
title: Get keys from a relational data base. — dbKeys
text: Function get primary foreign keys data frames relational data base.

title: Get keys from a relational data base. — dbKeys
Get keys from a relational data base. — dbKeys
text: filePath Path existing db file. includeMeta information Meta_Data table included.

title: Get keys from a relational data base. — dbKeys
text: Returns list named data tables db. elements contains list primary key, data table references corresponding foreign keys.

title: Get keys from a relational data base. — dbKeys
text: Data relational data base indexed primary foreign keys. Primary keys unique identifiers inside single data table. Foreign keys reference (link) data tables. function returns key structure relational data base.

title: Get keys from a relational data base. — dbKeys
code: db_path <- system.file("extdata", "example_dataBase.db", package = "eatDB") keys <- dbKeys(db_path)  ## primary key structure of the database keys$pkList #> $NoImp #> [1] "ID" #>  #> $Imp #> [1] "ID"  "imp" #>  #> $PVs #> [1] "ID"      "imp"     "subject" #>   ## foreign key structure of the database keys$fkList #> $NoImp #> $NoImp$References #> NULL #>  #> $NoImp$Keys #> NULL #>  #>  #> $Imp #> $Imp$References #> [1] "NoImp" #>  #> $Imp$Keys #> [1] "ID" #>  #>  #> $PVs #> $PVs$References #> [1] "Imp" #>  #> $PVs$Keys #> [1] "ID"  "imp" #>  #>

what: Get variable names from a relational data base. — dbNames
title: Get variable names from a relational data base. — dbNames
text: Function get names variables included relational data base.

title: Get variable names from a relational data base. — dbNames
Get variable names from a relational data base. — dbNames
text: filePath Path existing .db file. includeMeta variable names Meta_Data table included.

title: Get variable names from a relational data base. — dbNames
text: Returns named list character vectors variables names included data tables.

title: Get variable names from a relational data base. — dbNames
text: Extracts names variables included relational data base, structured list individual data tables elements. ordering list equivalent merge order used data pulled data base.

title: Get variable names from a relational data base. — dbNames
code: db_path <- system.file("extdata", "example_dataBase.db", package = "eatDB") varNames <- dbNames(db_path)  ## Names of data tables names(varNames) #> [1] "NoImp" "Imp"   "PVs"    ## Variable names in data table "NoImp" varNames$NoImp #> [1] "ID"     "age"    "weight"

what: Pull data from a relational data base. — dbPull
title: Pull data from a relational data base. — dbPull
text: Function extract specific variables various data tables. Variables merged specified merge order via left joins using foreign keys. variables selected specific data table, corresponding primary keys also always extracted. variables first data tables mergeOrder selected, data tables skipped (till first variable - data table match). variables single data table selected, data table extracted variables sub setting performed R.

title: Pull data from a relational data base. — dbPull
Pull data from a relational data base. — dbPull
text: vSelect Character vector variables pulled data base. vSelect NULL, variables data base selected. filePath Path existing db file.

title: Pull data from a relational data base. — dbPull
text: Returns data frame, including selected variables.

title: Pull data from a relational data base. — dbPull
text: Note exact merging process determined data base created via createDB can altered post hoc. options (e.g. filtering cases, full joins) still development. want use package specific requests, please contact package author.

title: Pull data from a relational data base. — dbPull
code: db_path <- system.file("extdata", "example_dataBase.db", package = "eatDB")  ## Extract variables from the first data table by name # primary and foreign keys are added as required dat1 <- dbPull(vSelect = c("age"), filePath = db_path)  ## Extract all variables from the first data table varNames <- dbNames(db_path) dat2 <- dbPull(vSelect = varNames$NoImp, filePath = db_path)  ## Extract variables from different data table (merged automatically) dat3 <- dbPull(vSelect = c("weight", "noBooks", "pv"), filePath = db_path)  ## Extract all variables from the data base dat4 <- dbPull(filePath = db_path)

what: Extract a single data table from a relational data base. — dbSingleDF
title: Extract a single data table from a relational data base. — dbSingleDF
text: Function extract single, complete data table relational data base. Especially useful extraction meta information stored Meta_Data.

title: Extract a single data table from a relational data base. — dbSingleDF
Extract a single data table from a relational data base. — dbSingleDF
text: dfName Name data table extracted. filePath Path existing db file.

title: Extract a single data table from a relational data base. — dbSingleDF
text: Returns data frame variables cases corresponding data table.

title: Extract a single data table from a relational data base. — dbSingleDF
text: function makes use DBI::dbReadTable function extracts complete data table data base. variables extracted rows used. extracting variables merging data tables see dbPull.

title: Extract a single data table from a relational data base. — dbSingleDF
code: db_path <- system.file("extdata", "example_dataBase.db", package = "eatDB")  ## Extract all meta information meta_data <- dbSingleDF(dfName = "Meta_Data", filePath = db_path) meta_data #>   varName                                                  varLabel data_table #> 1      ID                                               ID variable      NoImp #> 2     age                                              Age in years      NoImp #> 3  weight                                   Body weight in kilogram      NoImp #> 4     imp                                Multiple Imputation number        Imp #> 5 noBooks                   Number of books at home (self reported)        Imp #> 6 subject Competence domain (Mathematical Literacy/Reading Literacy        PVs #> 7      pv                                           Plausible value        PVs  ## Extract a specific data table NoImp <- dbSingleDF(dfName = "NoImp", filePath = db_path) NoImp #>   ID age weight #> 1  1  12     55 #> 2  2  17     40 #> 3  3  13     41 #> 4  4  15     42 #> 5  5  14     58

title: eatDB 0.5.0
text: CRAN release: 2021-10-05 dbPull() now uses dbSingleDF() internally, variables single data table selected (slightly increases memory usage cases guarantees correct output length speeds performance) Switched Github Action CI
